metadata.description: async/await: The `async function` returns a Promise. The `await` keyword pauses execution until the Promise resolves. Here is an example to illustrate this in Python-like pseudocode: ```python # Define an asynchronous function using async/await async def fetch_data(url): response = await get_request(url) # Await the promise returned by get_request return response.json() # Process the resolved value # Example usage of the async function result = fetch_data("https://api.example.com/data") print(result) # This will print the JSON data once the Promise is resolved ``` In this example, `fetch_data` is an asynchronous function that uses `await` to wait for a promise (in this case, returned by `get_request`) to resolve before proceeding. The result of the promise resolution is then processed and can be used in further operations.
